---
title: " "
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    css: custom.css
    slide-number: true
    controls: false
    menu: false
---

## { data-state="cover-slide" data-background-image="images/oslo_cover.png" data-background-size="cover" visibility="uncounted"}

## Motivation 

<div style="position:absolute; top:12%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-out">
  - [Evaluate dynamic predictions]{style="color:#ba1732;"} of imminent delivery ([7 days]{style="color:#ba1732;"}) in pregnancies complicated by early-onset fetal growth restriction.
  - [Main goal]{style="color:#ba1732;"} $\longrightarrow$ optimizing antenatal corticosteroid (CCS) administration
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:60%; left:12%;">
  <img src="images/time_line_mujer_feto.svg" style="width:90%; text-align:center;">
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:12%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in">
  - [OPTICORE]{style="color:#ba1732;"} data $\longrightarrow$ multicenter cohort study in the Netherlands
</div>


## Asymmetric misclassification 

<div style="position:absolute; top:9%; left:18%;">
  <img src="images/decision_tree.svg" style="width:80%; text-align:center;">
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:20%; left:78%;" class="fragment fade-in">
  [TP]{style="color:#228B22;"}
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:43%; left:78%;" class="fragment fade-in">
  [FP]{style="color:#FE8330;"}
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:66%; left:78%;" class="fragment fade-in">
  [TN]{style="color:#228B22;"}
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:90%; left:78%;" class="fragment fade-in">
  [FN]{style="color:#D30000;"}
</div>



## Definitions and notation

<div style="position:absolute; top:12%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
- $i=1,\dotsc,n$ individuals
-   $\require{color}\textcolor{#ba1732}{T_i^*}$ true time until event
-   $\require{color}\textcolor{#ba1732}{C_i}$ right-censoring times
-   $\require{color}\textcolor{#ba1732}{T_i=\min(T_i^*, C_i)}$ observed times
-   $\require{color}\textcolor{#ba1732}{\delta_i=\mathbf{1}\{T_i^*\leq C_i\}}$ censoring indicator
-   $\require{color}\textcolor{#ba1732}{L_i}$ left-truncation times
-   $\require{color}\textcolor{#ba1732}{\boldsymbol{\mathcal{H}}_i(t)}$ available history at time $t$
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:12%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in">
  - [Dynamic predictions]{style="color:#ba1732;"}
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:22%; left:9%;" class="fragment fade-in">
  $$
  \normalsize\require{color}
  \pi_{i}(s \mid t)=\operatorname{Pr}\left(T_{i}^* \leq t+s \mid T_{i}^*>t, \colorbox{#ba1732}{$\color{#ffffff}\boldsymbol{\mathcal{H}}_i(t)$}\right)
  $$
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:45%; left:12%;" class="fragment fade-in">
  <img src="images/dynamic_pred.svg" style="width:90%; text-align:center;">
</div>



## Scoring rules


<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:12%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  <img src="images/sr1.svg" style="width:90%; text-align:center;">
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:12%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  <img src="images/sr2.svg" style="width:90%; text-align:center;">
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:12%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  <img src="images/sr3.svg" style="width:90%; text-align:center;">
</div>


<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  - A scoring rule is [proper]{style="color:#ba1732;"} if 
  
  $$ E\left[\mathcal{S}\big(\pi_i^{\text{true}}(s\mid t), D_i(t,s)\big) \right] \leq E\left[\mathcal{S}\big(\widehat{\pi}_i(s\mid t), D_i(t,s)\big) \right]. $$
  
  - It is [strictly proper]{style="color:#ba1732;"} if it holds with equality if and only if $\pi_i^{\text{true}}(s\mid t)\equiv \widehat{\pi}_i(s\mid t)$.

<div style="height:10px;"></div>

  - It is [centered]{style="color:#ba1732;"} if $\mathcal{S}(1,1)=\mathcal{S}(0,0)=0$.
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:18%; left:10%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  <img src="images/table1.svg" style="width:100%; text-align:center;">
</div>


## Scoring rules: limitations 

<div style="height:15px;"></div>

- They quantify predictive accuracy, but [not the consequences of decisions based on predictions]{style="color:#ba1732;"}.

<div style="height:10px;"></div>

- They do not account for the [asymmetric harms of false-positives and false-negatives]{style="color:#ba1732;"} decisions.


## Clinical utility 

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">

<div style="height:15px;"></div>

  - Focus on the [quality of decisions]{style="color:#ba1732;"} driven by dynamic predictions.
  
<div style="height:10px;"></div>

  - Based on a clinically relevant [decision threshold]{style="color:#ba1732;"} $c\in(0,1)$.
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:15%; left:3%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  <img src="images/decision_rules.svg" style="width:100%; text-align:center;">
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  - Widely used clinical utility metric $\longrightarrow$ [net benefit (NB)]{style="color:#ba1732;"}
  - [Expected cost (EC)]{style="color:#ba1732;"}:
  
$$\normalsize\require{color}\begin{split}
        \begin{split}
        \text{EC}_c\big(\pi_i(s\mid t),\, & D_i(t,s)\big) =  c\mathbf{1}\{\pi_i(s\mid t)\geq c\} (1-D_i(t,s))\\
        &+(1-c)\mathbf{1}\{\pi_i(s\mid t)< c\}D_i(t,s)
    \end{split}
    \end{split} $$ 
</div>


<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:10%;" class="fragment fade-in">
  <img src="images/table2.svg" style="width:90%; text-align:center;">
</div>


<div style="position:absolute; top:80%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
- NB and EC rely on a [single decision threshold]{style="color:#ba1732;"}  $c\in (0,1)$
</div>




## Integrated weighted expected cost 

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  - To avoid reliance on a single $c$ and achieve strict properness:
  
  $$\normalsize\require{color}\begin{split} & \text{IWEC}_w \big(\pi_i(s\mid t), D_i(t,s)\big) = \\ & \int_0^1\text{EC}_c\big(\pi_i(s\mid t),D_i(t,s)\big)w(c)dc \end{split} $$
  
  - $w(\cdot)$ $\longrightarrow$ positive weight function reflecting the [clinical relevance of different $c$'s]{style="color:#ba1732;"}
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  - To avoid reliance on a single $c$ and achieve strict properness:
  
  $$\normalsize\require{color}\begin{split} & \text{IWEC}_w \big(\pi_i(s\mid t), D_i(t,s)\big) = \\ & \textcolor{#ba1732}{\int_0^1}\text{EC}_c\big(\pi_i(s\mid t),D_i(t,s)\big)\textcolor{#ba1732}{w(c)dc} \end{split} $$
  
  - $w(\cdot)$ $\longrightarrow$ positive weight function reflecting the [clinical relevance of different $c$'s]{style="color:#ba1732;"}
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  - To avoid reliance on a single $c$ and achieve strict properness:
  
  $$\normalsize\require{color}\begin{split} & \text{IWEC}_w \big(\pi_i(s\mid t), D_i(t,s)\big) = \\ & \textcolor{#ba1732}{\int_0^1}\text{EC}_c\big(\pi_i(s\mid t),D_i(t,s)\big)\textcolor{#ba1732}{w(c)dc} \end{split} $$
  
  - In practice $\longrightarrow$ [beta family]{style="color:#ba1732;"} of weight functions [$w(c;\alpha,\beta)$]{style="color:#ba1732;"}
</div>

::: {.fragment .fade-in-then-out}

<center>
<img src="images/table3.svg" width="75%">
</center>

- Weight function $\longrightarrow$ [all thresholds]{style="color:#BA1732;"} $c\in (0,1)$ and its [relative clinical importance]{style="color:#BA1732;"}.



:::

<div style="position:absolute; top:10%; left:12%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  <img src="images/ws_first.png" style="width:85%; text-align:center;">
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:10%; left:12%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  <img src="images/ws_shaded_area.png" style="width:85%; text-align:center;">
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:10%; left:12%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  <img src="images/w1_l.png" style="width:85%; text-align:center;">
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:10%; left:12%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  <img src="images/w2_l.png" style="width:85%; text-align:center;">
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:10%; left:12%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  <img src="images/w3_l.png" style="width:85%; text-align:center;">
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:10%; left:12%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  <img src="images/ws_l.png" style="width:85%; text-align:center;">
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in">
* A scoring rule is centered and strictly proper [$\Longleftrightarrow$]{style="color:#BA1732;"} it can be represented by $\text{IWEC}_w \big(\pi_i(s\mid t), D_i(t,s)\big)$ for some $w(\cdot)$.
  + [$w(c)=1$]{style="color:#BA1732;"} $\longrightarrow$ [Brier score]{style="color:#BA1732;"}
  + [$w(c)=c^{-1}(1-c)^{-1}$]{style="color:#BA1732;"} $\longrightarrow$  [logarithmic score]{style="color:#BA1732;"}
</div>





## Estimation under LTRC data 

* To [estimate]{style="color:#BA1732;"}:

$$\text{eIWEC}_w(t,s)=E\big[\text{IWEC}_w\big(\pi(s\mid t),D(t,s)\big) \mid T^*>t\big]$$

* We propose two approaches:
  + A nonparametric [IPW]{style="color:#BA1732;"} estimator.
  + A [model-based]{style="color:#BA1732;"} estimator.

* We established [consistency]{style="color:#BA1732;"} and [asymptotic normality]{style="color:#BA1732;"}.



## Simulation study 

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
* We [evaluate dynamic predictions]{style="color:#BA1732;"} within $(1,3]$ obtained via three Cox proportional-hazards models:
  + The true model $\longrightarrow$ [$M_\text{True}$]{style="color:#BA1732;"}
  + Two misspecified models $\longrightarrow$ [$M_1, M_2$]{style="color:#BA1732;"}
<div style="height:25px;"></div>
* To reflect the [asymmetry between FP and FN]{style="color:#BA1732;"} in our clinical context, we set [$c\in(0.1, 0.3)$]{style="color:#BA1732;"}.
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:10%; left:12%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  <img src="images/ws_simulstudy.png" style="width:85%; text-align:center;">
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:12%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in">
  <img src="images/plot_left.png" style="width:48%; text-align:center;">
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:12.1%; left:50%;" class="fragment fade-in">
  <img src="images/plot_right.png" style="width:150%; text-align:center;">
</div>



## Application: OPTICORE 

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
* [Goal]{style="color:#BA1732"} $\longrightarrow$ outperform current clinical practice for CCS administration.
* In the Netherlands:
  + [Strategy 1]{style="color:#BA1732"} $\longrightarrow$ when pulsatility index of the umbilical artery becomes abnormal
  + [Strategy 2]{style="color:#BA1732"} $\longrightarrow$ when an absent or reversed end-diastolic velocity of the umbilical artery is detected
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
- [Joint model]{style="color:#BA1732;"} considering four different fetal outcomes:

$$\scriptsize\begin{split} 
\textcolor{#BA1732}{\text{JM}}:  h_{i}\left(t \mid \boldsymbol{\mathcal{H}}_{i}(t), \boldsymbol{X}_{i}\right) =h_{0}(t) \exp \left(  \gamma\boldsymbol{X}_{i} + \sum_{j=1}^4\alpha_j f_j\{Y_{ji}(t)\} \right) 
\end{split}$$

- [Dynamic predictions]{style="color:#BA1732;"} $\longrightarrow$  $(182, 189]$ days = $(26, 27]$ weeks of gestation. 
- We consider [$c\in(0.1,0.3)$]{style="color:#BA1732;"} and [$w(c;5.16, 17.62)$]{style="color:#BA1732;"}.
</div>


<div style="position:absolute; top:12%; left:8%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  <img src="images/wdcs1.png" style="width:100%; text-align:center;">
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:12%; left:8%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  <img src="images/wdcs2.png" style="width:100%; text-align:center;">
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:12%; left:8%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  <img src="images/areas1.png" style="width:100%; text-align:center;">
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:12%; left:8%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  <img src="images/areas2.png" style="width:100%; text-align:center;">
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:12%; left:8%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  <img src="images/areas3.png" style="width:100%; text-align:center;">
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:12%; left:8%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  <img src="images/areas4.png" style="width:100%; text-align:center;">
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:12%; left:8%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
  <img src="images/areas5.png" style="width:100%; text-align:center;">
</div>


## Contribution 

::: {.fragment .fade-in-then-out}

- [Extension of clinical utility]{style="color:#BA1732;"} to dynamic prediction.
- [IWEC]{style="color:#BA1732;"} and [weighted decision curves]{style="color:#BA1732;"}.
- [A comprehensive estimation framework]{style="color:#BA1732;"} for [LTRC data]{style="color:#BA1732;"}. 
- Methods will be available in the [JMbayes2 R package]{style="color:#BA1732;"}.

<center>
<img src="images/JMBayes2-logo-no-background.gif" width="23%">
</center>

:::

## Limitations & future research

- Limitations $\longrightarrow$ Reducing the time-to-event outcome to a [binary outcome]{style="color:#BA1732;"} discards relevant information. 

<div style="height:30px;"></div>

- Future work $\longrightarrow$ Identifying the best-performing [joint model]{style="color:#BA1732;"} and translating it into an [online tool for obstetricians]{style="color:#BA1732;"} to generate dynamic risk predictions for delivery.



## { data-state="cover-slide" data-background-image="images/oslo_contra.png" data-background-size="cover" visibility="uncounted"}


## Challenges of the data {visibility="uncounted"}

-   CCS cannot be administered until the fetus reaches [$500$ grams]{style="color:#BA1732;"} and a gestational age of [$168$ days]{style="color:#BA1732;"} ($24$ weeks).

<div style="position:absolute; top:37%; left:12%;">
  <img src="images/LT2.svg" style="width:90%; text-align:center;">
</div>

## Challenges of the data {visibility="uncounted"}

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
- Under LTRC data $D_i(t,s)=\mathbf{1}\{t<T_i^*\leq t+s\}$ is [unobserved]{style="color:#BA1732;"} for $i$ censored within $(t, t+s]$ or $L_i>t$.

<img src="images/ltrc1.svg" style="width:63%; display:block; margin:auto;">

</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
- Under LTRC data $D_i(t,s)=\mathbf{1}\{t<T_i^*\leq t+s\}$ is [unobserved]{style="color:#BA1732;"} for $i$ censored within $(t, t+s]$ or $L_i>t$.

<img src="images/ltrc2.png" style="width:63%; display:block; margin:auto;">

</div>

## IPW estimator {visibility="uncounted"}

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">

* We define the [IPW estimator]{style="color:#BA1732;"}:

$$\scriptsize\require{color} \widehat{\text{eIWEC}}^{\text{IPW}}_w(t,s)=\frac{1}{n_t} \sum_{i:L_i<t<T_i} \widehat{W}_i(t,s)\text{IWEC}_w\big(\pi_i(s\mid t),\tilde{D}_i(t,s)\big)$$


  + $n_t$ $\longrightarrow$ number of individuals at risk at $t$
  + $\tilde{D}_i(t,s)$ $\longrightarrow$ the observed binary indicator
  + $\widehat{W}_i(t,s)$  $\longrightarrow$ adjust for LTRC data via left-truncated Kaplan--Meier of the censoring distribution

</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">

* We define the [IPW estimator]{style="color:#BA1732;"}:

$$ \scriptsize\require{color}\widehat{\text{eIWEC}}^{\text{IPW}}_w(t,s)=\frac{1}{\textcolor{#BA1732}{n_t}} \sum_{i:L_i<t<T_i} \widehat{W}_i(t,s)\text{IWEC}_w\big(\pi_i(s\mid t),\tilde{D}_i(t,s)\big)$$


  + $\textcolor{#BA1732}{n_t}$ $\longrightarrow$  number of individuals at risk at $t$
  + $\tilde{D}_i(t,s)$ $\longrightarrow$  the observed binary indicator
  + $\widehat{W}_i(t,s)$ $\longrightarrow$  adjust for LTRC data via left-truncated Kaplan--Meier of the censoring distribution
  

</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">

* We define the [IPW estimator]{style="color:#BA1732;"}:

$$\scriptsize\require{color} \widehat{\text{eIWEC}}^{\text{IPW}}_w(t,s)=\frac{1}{n_t} \sum_{i:L_i<t<T_i} \widehat{W}_i(t,s)\text{IWEC}_w\big(\pi_i(s\mid t),\textcolor{#BA1732}{\tilde{D}_i(t,s)}\big)$$


  + $n_t$ $\longrightarrow$  number of individuals at risk at $t$
  + $\textcolor{#BA1732}{\tilde{D}_i(t,s)}$ $\longrightarrow$  the observed binary indicator
  + $\widehat{W}_i(t,s)$ $\longrightarrow$ adjust for LTRC data via left-truncated Kaplan--Meier of the censoring distribution

</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">

* We define the [IPW estimator]{style="color:#BA1732;"}:

$$\scriptsize\require{color} \widehat{\text{eIWEC}}^{\text{IPW}}_w(t,s)=\frac{1}{n_t} \sum_{i:L_i<t<T_i} \textcolor{#BA1732}{\widehat{W}_i(t,s)}\text{IWEC}_w\big(\pi_i(s\mid t),\tilde{D}_i(t,s)\big)$$


  + $n_t$ $\longrightarrow$  number of individuals at risk at $t$
  + $\tilde{D}_i(t,s)$ $\longrightarrow$  the observed binary indicator
  + $\textcolor{#BA1732}{\widehat{W}_i(t,s)}$ $\longrightarrow$  adjust for LTRC data via left-truncated Kaplan--Meier of the censoring distribution

</div>




## Model-based estimator {visibility="uncounted"}

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">

* We define the [model-based estimator]{style="color:#BA1732;"}:

$$\scriptsize\require{color}\begin{split}
        \widehat{\text{eIWEC}}^{\text{MB}}_w(t,s)=  \frac{1}{n_t}&\sum_{i=1}^n  \delta_i\mathbf{1}\{L_i<t<T_i\leq t+s\}\text{IWEC}_w\{\pi_i(s\mid t),1\} \\
        &+ \mathbf{1}\{T_i>t+s\}\mathbf{1}\{L_i<t\}\text{IWEC}_w\{\pi_i(s\mid t),0\} \\
        & + (1-\delta_i)\mathbf{1}\{L_i<t<T_i\leq t+s\}\left[\pi_i(s\mid t)\text{IWEC}_w\{\pi_i(s\mid t),1\} \\
        + (1-\pi_i(s\mid t))\text{IWEC}_w\{\pi_i(s\mid t),0\} \right].
    \end{split}$$



</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">

* We define the [model-based estimator]{style="color:#BA1732;"}:

$$\scriptsize\require{color}\begin{split}
        \widehat{\text{eIWEC}}^{\text{MB}}_w(t,s)=  \frac{1}{n_t}&\sum_{i=1}^n  \textcolor{#BA1732}{\delta_i\mathbf{1}\{L_i<t<T_i\leq t+s\}}\text{IWEC}_w\{\pi_i(s\mid t),\textcolor{#BA1732}{1}\} \\
        &+ \mathbf{1}\{T_i>t+s\}\mathbf{1}\{L_i<t\}\text{IWEC}_w\{\pi_i(s\mid t),0\} \\
        & + (1-\delta_i)\mathbf{1}\{L_i<t<T_i\leq t+s\}\left[\pi_i(s\mid t)\text{IWEC}_w\{\pi_i(s\mid t),1\} \\
        + (1-\pi_i(s\mid t))\text{IWEC}_w\{\pi_i(s\mid t),0\} \right].
    \end{split}$$


</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">

* We define the [model-based estimator]{style="color:#BA1732;"}:

$$\scriptsize\require{color}\begin{split}
        \widehat{\text{eIWEC}}^{\text{MB}}_w(t,s)=  \frac{1}{n_t}&\sum_{i=1}^n  \delta_i\mathbf{1}\{L_i<t<T_i\leq t+s\}\text{IWEC}_w\{\pi_i(s\mid t),1\} \\
        &+ \textcolor{#BA1732}{\mathbf{1}\{T_i>t+s\}\mathbf{1}\{L_i<t\}}\text{IWEC}_w\{\pi_i(s\mid t),\textcolor{#BA1732}{0}\} \\
        & + (1-\delta_i)\mathbf{1}\{L_i<t<T_i\leq t+s\}\left[\pi_i(s\mid t)\text{IWEC}_w\{\pi_i(s\mid t),1\} \\
        + (1-\pi_i(s\mid t))\text{IWEC}_w\{\pi_i(s\mid t),0\} \right].
    \end{split}$$


</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">

* We define the [model-based estimator]{style="color:#BA1732;"}:

$$\scriptsize\require{color}\begin{split}
        \widehat{\text{eIWEC}}^{\text{MB}}_w(t,s)=  \frac{1}{n_t}&\sum_{i=1}^n  \delta_i\mathbf{1}\{L_i<t<T_i\leq t+s\}\text{IWEC}_w\{\pi_i(s\mid t),1\} \\
        &+ \mathbf{1}\{T_i>t+s\}\mathbf{1}\{L_i<t\}\text{IWEC}_w\{\pi_i(s\mid t),0\} \\
        & + \textcolor{#BA1732}{(1-\delta_i)\mathbf{1}\{L_i<t<T_i\leq t+s\}}\left[\textcolor{#BA1732}{\pi_i(s\mid t)}\text{IWEC}_w\{\pi_i(s\mid t),\textcolor{#BA1732}{1}\} \\
        + \textcolor{#BA1732}{(1-\pi_i(s\mid t))}\text{IWEC}_w\{\pi_i(s\mid t),\textcolor{#BA1732}{0}\} \right].
    \end{split}$$


</div>

## Large sample properties  {visibility="uncounted"}

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in">
* We demonstrated that both the IPW and model-based estimators are:
  + [Consistent]{style="color:#BA1732;"}
  + [Asymptotically normal]{style="color:#BA1732;"} 
</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:53%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in">
* This result applies to [all centered strictly proper metrics]{style="color:#BA1732;"}:
  + [Brier score]{style="color:#BA1732;"}
  + [Logarithmic score]{style="color:#BA1732;"} 
</div>

## The joint model

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
- [Linear mixed-effects]{style="color:#BA1732;"} submodels:

$$ \require{color} Y_{ji}(t) = m_{ji}(t) + \varepsilon_{ji}(t), \quad j=1,\dots, 4 $$

- $m_{ji}(t)$ $\longrightarrow$ natural cubic splines
- $\varepsilon_{ji}(t)\sim N(0,\sigma^2)$

</div>

<div style="position:absolute; top:13%; left:0%;" class="fragment fade-in-then-out">
- [Linear mixed-effects]{style="color:#BA1732;"} submodels:

$$ \require{color} Y_{ji}(t) = \colorbox{#BA1732}{$\color{#ffffff}m_{1i}(t)$} + \varepsilon_{1i}(t), \quad j=1,\dots, 4 $$
</div>
